Fix: Investigate the deadlock, fix the underlying issue, and re-enable the test.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-16T18:17:22.103838-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b3og","depends_on_id":"bd-tggf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T18:19:05.740642-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}],"deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}
 {"id":"bd-b6xo","title":"Remove or fix ClearDirtyIssues() - race condition risk (bd-52)","description":"Code health review found internal/storage/sqlite/dirty.go still exposes old ClearDirtyIssues() method (lines 103-108) which clears ALL dirty issues without checking what was actually exported.

Data loss risk: If export fails after some issues written to JSONL but before ClearDirtyIssues called, changes to remaining dirty issues will be lost.

The safer ClearDirtyIssuesByID() (lines 113-132) exists and clears only exported issues.

Fix: Either remove old method or mark it deprecated and ensure no code paths use it.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-16T18:17:20.534625-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b6xo","depends_on_id":"bd-tggf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T18:19:05.633738-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}],"deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}

 {"id":"bd-bha9","title":"Add missing updated_at index on issues table","description":"GetStaleIssues queries filter by updated_at but there's no index on this column.

**Current query (ready.go:253-254):**
```sql
WHERE status != 'closed'
  AND datetime(updated_at) < datetime('now', '-' || ? || ' days')
```

**Problem:** Full table scan when filtering stale issues.

**Solution:** Add migration to create:
```sql
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_updated_at ON issues(updated_at);
```

**Note:** The datetime() function wrapper may prevent index usage. Consider also storing updated_at as INTEGER (unix timestamp) for better index efficiency, or test if SQLite can use the index despite the function.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:49.166051-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T23:15:13.837078-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T23:15:13.837078-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-bha9","depends_on_id":"bd-h0we","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:49.166949-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-gigi","title":"bd admin compact --older-than=0 ignores the flag, uses default 30 days","description":"When running bd admin compact --prune --older-than=0, the output says 'No expired tombstones to prune (TTL: 30 days)' - the flag value is ignored.

Root cause: In runCompactPrune() in compact_tombstone.go:180, the check 'if compactOlderThan > 0' means that --older-than=0 is treated the same as not passing the flag, defaulting to 30 days.

Fix: Change the default flag value in compact.go:785 to -1 (sentinel for 'use default'), and treat 0 as 'expire all tombstones' by passing a negative TTL to pruneExpiredTombstones().

Moved from gt-65gwa (gastown) - was filed in wrong rig.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"beads/crew/emma","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:34:41.255352-08:00","created_by":"gastown/polecats/dementus","updated_at":"2025-12-30T00:00:43.476085-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T00:00:43.476085-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed: flag default changed to -1 sentinel, 0 now means expire all"}

 {"id":"bd-jke6","title":"Add covering index (label, issue_id) for label queries","description":"GetIssuesByLabel joins labels table but requires table lookup after using idx_labels_label.

**Query (labels.go:165):**
```sql
SELECT ... FROM issues i
JOIN labels l ON i.id = l.issue_id
WHERE l.label = ?
```

**Problem:** Current idx_labels_label index doesn't cover issue_id, requiring row lookup.

**Solution:** Add migration:
```sql
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_labels_label_issue ON labels(label, issue_id);
```

This is a covering index - query can be satisfied entirely from the index without touching the labels table rows.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:51.485354-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T23:15:13.839904-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T23:15:13.839904-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-jke6","depends_on_id":"bd-h0we","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:51.485984-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

Need to make protection timestamp-aware: only protect if local is newer than incoming. See issue for detailed analysis and fix options.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"beads/crew/emma","created_at":"2026-01-03T13:12:54.354717-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/grip","updated_at":"2026-01-03T13:17:43.985187-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-jsk7","title":"Agent beads: structured labels for filtering","description":"## Agent Beads Need Queryable Labels

Currently agent beads have role_type/rig in description text, not as labels. This breaks @group resolution in gt mail.

## Current State
```json
{
  "id": "gt-gastown-witness",
  "issue_type": "agent",
  "description": "...\\nrole_type: witness\\nrig: gastown\\n..."
}
```

Cannot query: `bd list --type=agent --label=role_type:witness` returns nothing.

## Required
Agent bead creation should add labels:
- `role_type:<type>` (witness, refinery, crew, polecat, dog, mayor, deacon)
- `rig:<rig>` (gastown, beads, or "town" for town-level)

## Where to Fix
gt polecat/crew/agent creation commands should add labels.

## Acceptance
- New agent beads created with role_type/rig labels
- @group patterns work in gt mail router","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T20:49:13.444793-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/joe","updated_at":"2025-12-29T20:58:04.67866-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T20:58:04.67866-08:00","close_reason":"Duplicate of bd-g7eq"}
 {"id":"bd-jv4w","title":"Phase 1.2: Separate bdt executable - Initial structure","description":"Create minimal bdt command structure completely separate from bd. Must not share code, config, or database.

## Subtasks
1. Create cmd/bdt/ directory with main.go
2. Implement bdt version, help, and status commands
3. Configure separate database location: $HOME/.bdt/ (not $HOME/.beads/)
4. Create separate issues file: issues.toon (not issues.jsonl)
5. Update build system:
   - Makefile: Add bdt target
   - .goreleaser.yml: Add bdt binary config

## Files to Create
- cmd/bdt/main.go - Entry point
- cmd/bdt/version.go - Version handling
- cmd/bdt/help.go - Help text (separate from bd)

## Success Criteria
- `make build` produces both `bd` and `bdt` executables
- `bdt version` shows distinct version output from bd
- `bdt --help` shows distinct help text
- bdt uses $HOME/.bdt/ directory (verify with `bdt info`)
- bd and bdt completely isolated (no shared imports beyond stdlib)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T11:48:34.866877282-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T12:59:11.389296015-07:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T12:59:11.389296015-07:00"}

 {"id":"bd-obep","title":"Spawn-time bonding: --attach flag","description":"Add --attach flag to bd mol spawn for on-the-fly composition.

COMMAND: bd mol spawn proto-feature --attach proto-docs --attach proto-testing

BEHAVIOR:
- Spawn the primary proto as normal
- For each --attach: spawn that proto and wire to primary
- Attachments become children of primary's root epic
- Dependencies wired based on bond type (default: sequential)

FLAGS:
- --attach PROTO: Attach a proto (can repeat)
- --attach-type TYPE: sequential (default) or parallel for all attachments
- --after ISSUE: Attachment point for attached protos

VARIABLE HANDLING:
- All attached protos share variable namespace
- Warn on variable name conflicts
- All --var flags apply to all protos","notes":"DESIGN NOTE: This is syntactic sugar. Equivalent to:
  bd mol spawn proto-A
  bd mol bond $new_epic_id proto-B
  
Keeping as separate task because it's a common UX pattern worth optimizing.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:59:06.178092-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T10:42:50.554816-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T10:42:50.554816-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-obep","depends_on_id":"bd-o5xe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:59:51.368491-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-obep","depends_on_id":"bd-o91r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:59:51.733369-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-of2p","title":"Improve version bump molecule with missing steps","description":"During v0.32.1 release, discovered missing steps in the release molecule:

**Missing from molecule:**
1. Rebuild ~/go/bin/bd (only did ~/.local/bin/bd)
2. Install beads-mcp from local source: `uv tool install --reinstall ./integrations/beads-mcp`
3. Restart daemons with `bd daemons killall`
4. (Optional) Publish beads-mcp to PyPI

**Current molecule steps (bd-6s61):**
1. Update CHANGELOG.md
2. Update info.go versionChanges  
3. Run bump-version.sh
4. Run tests and linting
5. Update local installation
6. Commit and push release
7. Wait for CI
8. Verify release artifacts

**Proposed additions:**
- After "Update local installation": rebuild BOTH ~/.local/bin/bd AND ~/go/bin/bd
- Add: "Install beads-mcp from source" step
- Add: "Restart daemons" step
- Add: "Verify all versions match" step that checks all artifacts

**Also learned:**
- Must run from mayor/rig to avoid git conflicts with bd sync (already documented in bump-version.sh)","notes":"CORRECTION: npm publishing IS automated and working!

**Package naming:**
- OLD: `beads` (npm) - deprecated, stuck at 0.2.1
- CURRENT: `@beads/bd` (npm) - scoped package, auto-published by CI

**How it works:**
- CI uses OIDC trusted publishing (no token needed)
- Workflow: .github/workflows/release.yml → publish-npm job
- Permissions: `id-token: write` enables GitHub OIDC
- To install: `npm install -g @beads/bd` (not `npm install beads`)

**All publishing is automated on tag push:**
1. GitHub Release - goreleaser ✓
2. PyPI - publish-pypi job ✓
3. Homebrew - update-homebrew job ✓
4. npm (@beads/bd) - publish-npm job ✓

**Remaining molecule improvements (local steps only):**
- Rebuild BOTH ~/.local/bin/bd AND ~/go/bin/bd
- Install beads-mcp from source: `uv tool install --reinstall ./integrations/beads-mcp`
- Restart daemons: `bd daemons killall`
- Run from mayor/rig to avoid git conflicts with bd sync
- Final verification step to check all local versions match","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T22:09:11.845787-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T16:01:18.199132-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T16:01:18.199132-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-sj5y","title":"Daemon should be singleton and aggressively kill stale instances","description":"Found 2 bd daemons running (PIDs 76868, 77515) during shutdown. The daemon should:

1. Be a singleton - only one instance per rig allowed
2. On startup, check for existing daemon and kill it before starting
3. Use a PID file or lock file to enforce this

Currently stale daemons can accumulate, causing confusion and resource waste.","notes":"**Investigation 2025-12-21:**

The singleton mechanism is already implemented and working correctly:

1. **daemon.lock** uses flock (exclusive non-blocking) to prevent duplicate daemons
2. **bd.sock.startlock** coordinates concurrent auto-starts via O_CREATE|O_EXCL
3. **Registry** tracks all daemons globally in ~/.beads/registry.json

Testing shows:
- Trying to start a second daemon gives: 'Error: daemon already running (PID X)'
- Multiple daemons for *different* rigs is expected/correct behavior

The original report ('Found 2 bd daemons running PIDs 76868, 77515') was likely:
1. Two daemons for different rigs (expected), OR
2. An edge case that's since been fixed

Consider closing as RESOLVED or clarifying the original scenario.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T01:29:14.778949-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T11:27:34.302585-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T11:27:34.302585-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-soig","title":"Sling reports 'already pinned' but mol status shows empty hook","description":"During swarm operations, gt sling reported beads were 'already pinned' but gt mol status showed nothing on the hook.

**Observed:**
```
$ gt sling bd-9btu beads/Nux
Error: bead bd-9btu is already pinned to gt-beads-nux

$ gt mol status beads/Nux
Nothing on hook - no work slung
```

**Expected:**
If a bead is pinned, mol status should show it. If mol status shows empty, sling should work.

**Workaround:**
Used --force flag to re-sling, which worked.

**Root cause hypothesis:**
Agent bead state may be stored in town beads but polecat's local view doesn't see it, or the pin record exists but session state is stale.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-28T16:17:50.561021-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/dave","updated_at":"2025-12-28T22:14:22.838484-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-28T22:14:22.838484-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-ykd9","title":"Add bd doctor --fix flag to automatically repair issues","description":"Add bd doctor --fix flag to automatically repair detected issues.

## Current State
- Doctor checks return issues with "Fix" field containing fix instructions
- No automatic fix execution
- User must manually follow fix instructions

## Implementation

### 1. Add --fix flag to doctor.go
```go
// In cmd/bd/doctor.go init()
doctorCmd.Flags().Bool("fix", false, "Automatically fix detected issues")
doctorCmd.Flags().Bool("yes", false, "Skip confirmation prompts (use with --fix)")
```

### 2. Create fix registry (cmd/bd/doctor/fix/registry.go)
```go
package fix

// Fixer can automatically repair an issue
type Fixer interface {
    // CanFix returns true if this fixer handles the given check name
    CanFix(checkName string) bool
    // Fix attempts to repair the issue, returns error if failed
    Fix(ctx context.Context, issue *CheckResult) error
    // Description returns human-readable description of what will be fixed
    Description() string
}

var registry []Fixer

func Register(f Fixer) {
    registry = append(registry, f)
}

func GetFixer(checkName string) Fixer {
    for _, f := range registry {
        if f.CanFix(checkName) {
            return f
        }
    }
    return nil
}
```

### 3. Implement fixers (one per file)

**cmd/bd/doctor/fix/hooks.go**
```go
type HooksFixer struct{}

func (f *HooksFixer) CanFix(name string) bool {
    return name == "git-hooks" || name == "hooks-outdated"
}

func (f *HooksFixer) Fix(ctx context.Context, issue *CheckResult) error {
    return hooks.Install(".", true) // force reinstall
}

func (f *HooksFixer) Description() string {
    return "Reinstall git hooks"
}

func init() {
    Register(&HooksFixer{})
}
```

**cmd/bd/doctor/fix/sync_branch.go**
```go
type SyncBranchFixer struct{}

func (f *SyncBranchFixer) CanFix(name string) bool {
    return name == "sync-branch-missing" || name == "sync-branch-diverged"
}

func (f *SyncBranchFixer) Fix(ctx context.Context, issue *CheckResult) error {
    // Reset to remote or create branch
    return syncbranch.ResetToRemote(ctx, repoRoot, branch, jsonlPath)
}
```

**cmd/bd/doctor/fix/merge_driver.go**
```go
type MergeDriverFixer struct{}

func (f *MergeDriverFixer) CanFix(name string) bool {
    return name == "merge-driver-missing" || name == "merge-driver-outdated"
}

func (f *MergeDriverFixer) Fix(ctx context.Context, issue *CheckResult) error {
    return setupMergeDriver()
}
```

### 4. Update doctor run logic

```go
func runDoctor(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) {
    issues := runAllChecks()
    
    if \!fixFlag {
        // Existing behavior - just display issues
        displayIssues(issues)
        return
    }
    
    // Collect fixable issues
    var fixable []FixableIssue
    for _, issue := range issues {
        if fixer := fix.GetFixer(issue.CheckName); fixer \!= nil {
            fixable = append(fixable, FixableIssue{issue, fixer})
        }
    }
    
    if len(fixable) == 0 {
        fmt.Println("No automatically fixable issues found")
        return
    }
    
    // Show what will be fixed
    fmt.Printf("Found %d fixable issues:\n", len(fixable))
    for i, f := range fixable {
        fmt.Printf("  %d. [%s] %s\n", i+1, f.Issue.CheckName, f.Fixer.Description())
    }
    
    // Confirm unless --yes
    if \!yesFlag {
        fmt.Print("\nProceed with fixes? [Y/n] ")
        // ... read confirmation
    }
    
    // Apply fixes
    for _, f := range fixable {
        fmt.Printf("Fixing %s... ", f.Issue.CheckName)
        if err := f.Fixer.Fix(ctx, f.Issue); err \!= nil {
            fmt.Printf("FAILED: %v\n", err)
        } else {
            fmt.Println("OK")
        }
    }
}
```

## Fixable Checks (Initial Set)

| Check | Fixer | Action |
|-------|-------|--------|
| git-hooks | HooksFixer | Reinstall hooks |
| hooks-outdated | HooksFixer | Update hooks |
| merge-driver-missing | MergeDriverFixer | Configure merge driver |
| sync-branch-diverged | SyncBranchFixer | Reset to remote |
| daemon-version-mismatch | DaemonFixer | Restart daemon |

## Testing
```bash
# Test with broken hooks
rm .git/hooks/pre-commit
bd doctor --fix --yes

# Verify fix applied
bd doctor  # Should pass now
```

## Success Criteria
- --fix flag triggers automatic repair
- User prompted for confirmation (unless --yes)
- Clear output showing what was fixed
- Graceful handling of fix failures
- At least 5 common issues auto-fixable","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-14T18:17:48.411264-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:34:05.646445-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T13:34:05.646445-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ykd9","depends_on_id":"bd-iz5t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T12:44:07.732505-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ykqu","title":"Add gate timeout tracking and notification","description":"Implement timeout and notification logic for gates.

## Timeout Behavior
1. Gate created with timeout (e.g., 30m)
2. Deacon tracks elapsed time during patrol
3. If timeout reached:
   - Notify all waiters: "Gate timed out"
   - Close gate with timeout reason
   - Waiter can retry, escalate, or fail gracefully

## Notification
- Use gt mail send to notify waiters
- Include gate ID, await type, and reason in message
- Support multiple waiters notification

## Escalation Path
- Witness sees stuck worker, nudges them
- Worker can escalate to human if needed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:40.1825-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:44.362527-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:44.362527-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ykqu","depends_on_id":"bd-udsi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:53.072862-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-ykqu","depends_on_id":"bd-is6m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:56.595085-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-yuxq","title":"Code smell: 262 uses of interface{} reduce type safety","description":"Heavy use of interface{} across 92 files (262 occurrences) in:
- RPC update arguments: updatesFromArgs() returns map[string]interface{}
- JSON unmarshaling and formatting
- Storage query helpers

**Example in internal/rpc/server_issues_epics.go:45:**
```go
func updatesFromArgs(a UpdateArgs) map[string]interface{} {
    // Returns map with interface{} values - types must be asserted at use sites
}
```

**Problem:**
- Loss of type safety
- Runtime type assertions required
- Harder to catch errors at compile time

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Define typed update structs for specific field groups
- [ ] Use generics where possible (Go 1.18+)
- [ ] Reduce interface{} count by at least 50%
- [ ] Document expected types where interface{} remains necessary
- [ ] Tests pass","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-12-28T19:00:01.896147-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/dave","updated_at":"2025-12-30T15:44:43.347739-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T07:04:41.376731-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented type safety improvements:

1. Created IssueUpdate struct with typed fields and ToMap() for backward compatibility
2. Created 15+ typed response structs (StatusMessage, DeleteResult, CompactResult, etc.)
3. Refactored updatesFromArgs to use typed IssueUpdate internally
4. Updated RPC delete handler to use typed DeleteResult
5. Updated migrate.go to use StatusMessage for errors

The typed structs provide compile-time checking and reduce runtime type assertions. This foundation enables further cleanup of interface{} usage over time.

Note: Many remaining interface{} usages are inherent to Go's JSON handling and external APIs (GraphQL, SQL). The acceptance criteria of 50% reduction applies to the refactorable patterns, not the total count.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yuxq","depends_on_id":"bd-ox1o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-28T19:00:23.812412-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
